name: Stephanie Schriock, EMILY's List
subject: Can't stop us >>
body:
We elect pro-choice Democratic women who will fight for women and families. | [ ](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ca0/24c69fd9/16c6f8316/189eab64/1359044642/VEsH/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/donate-to-emily?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=1+-+%0D%0A++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++&utm_campaign=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact&source=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact")
---

Peter,

What a difference a year makes.

Last year, we were mourning the results of the 2016 election. Today, we are celebrating a Democratic sweep in state and local elections across the country.

That difference comes down to millions of people across this country deciding to get involved. And, wow, did this community step up big time in the last 365 days.

Since Election Day 2016, more than 20,000 women have told us they want to run for office. More than 8,000 people have told us they want to help those women win. We launched our biggest Republican opposition program ever. And on Election Day 2017, we won 32 races together.

We have come _so far_ in a year. And we're not slowing down now.

**[Donate $3 to keep our movement going strong as we resist by electing pro-choice Democratic women to every level of government.](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ca0/24c69fd9/16c6f8316/189eab61/1359044642/VEsA/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171108_email_OneYearSince?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=6+-+Donate+3+to+keep+our+movement+going+stro&utm_campaign=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact&source=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact")**

We are certainly in an unprecedented moment in our nation's history.

But this community's unprecedented response is what helped Democrats win up and down the ballot on Tuesday night, particularly diverse Democratic women. Thanks to this community's help, the first Latinas, transgender woman, and Vietnamese American will serve in the Virginia House of Delegates next year.

We have so much to be proud of.

And I am fired up thinking about all that we can accomplish in the next year, too -- and have no doubt, 2018 will be a big year for us. Whether it's our slate of _**11**_ U.S. Senate candidates, the possibility of flipping the House, or electing a record number of women to state and local office, we are on the precipice of monumental change.

But we also know we can't get there without hard work. So, if you are still fired up to resist, persist, and show what women can do...

**Please donate $3 to help train and build winning campaigns for the next generation of women lawmakers:**

**[https://secure.emilyslist.org/Resist-Persist-Fight-Back](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ca0/24c69fd9/16c6f8316/189eab9e/1359044642/VEsHBA/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171108_email_OneYearSince?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=11+-+httpssecureemilyslistorgResistPersistFig&utm_campaign=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact&source=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact")**

Thanks for being in the fight,

Stephanie Schriock
President, EMILY's List

**[](https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/m/118c6ca0/24c69fd9/16c6f8316/189eab9b/1359044642/VEsHAw/ "https://secure.emilyslist.org/page/contribute/20171108_email_OneYearSince?utm_medium=email&utm_source=emilyslist&utm_content=16&utm_campaign=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact&source=WQEFEK18N_digital_email_20171109_OneYearSince_inact")**

name: Jason Yates, My Faith Votes
subject: Remembering Veterans on this day.
body:
| |
---
[ ](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=http%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171110_vet_s1&n=1)
| On this Veterans Day, My Faith Votes would like to thank all who have served and are serving our great country and share the speech given by President Ronald Reagan, on November 11th, 1985. _Secretary Weinberger, Harry Walters, Robert Medairos, reverend clergy, ladies and gentlemen, a few moments ago I placed a wreath at the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier, and as I stepped back and stood during the moment of silence that followed, I said a small prayer. And it occurred to me that each of my predecessors has had a similar moment, and I wondered if our prayers weren't very much the same, if not identical._ _We celebrate Veterans Day on the anniversary of the armistice that ended World War I, the armistice that began on the 11th hour of the 11th day of the 11th month. And I wonder, in fact, if all Americans' prayers aren't the same as those I mentioned a moment ago. The timing of this holiday is quite deliberate in terms of historical fact but somehow it always seems quite fitting to me that this day comes deep in autumn when the colors are muted and the days seem to invite contemplation._
* * *
| | [Watch Original Speech ►](http://www2.myfaithvotes.org/r?u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.myfaithvotes.org%2Fronald-reagans-1985-veterans-day-speech&e=be53806c85fc17bd685e1e4c4d896e92&utm_source=myfaithvotes&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=171110_vet_s1&n=2)
---
* * *
_We are gathered at the National Cemetery, which provides a final resting place for the heroes who have defended our country since the Civil War. This amphitheater, this place for speeches, is more central to this cemetery than it first might seem apparent, for all we can ever do for our heroes is remember them and remember what they did -- and memories are transmitted through words._
_Sometime back I received in the name of our country the bodies of four marines who had died while on active duty. I said then that there is a special sadness that accompanies the death of a serviceman, for we're never quite good enough to them-not really; we can't be, because what they gave us is beyond our powers to repay. And so, when a serviceman dies, it's a tear in the fabric, a break in the whole, and all we can do is remember._
_It is, in a way, an odd thing to honor those who died in defense of our country, in defense of us, in wars far away. The imagination plays a trick. We see these soldiers in our mind as old and wise. We see them as something like the Founding Fathers, grave and gray haired. But most of them were boys when they died, and they gave up two lives -- the one they were living and the one they would have lived. When they died, they gave up their chance to be husbands and fathers and grandfathers. They gave up their chance to be revered old men. They gave up everything for our country, for us. And all we can do is remember._
_There's always someone who is remembering for us. No matter what time of year it is or what time of day, there are always people who come to this cemetery, leave a flag or a flower or a little rock on a headstone. And they stop and bow their heads and communicate what they wished to communicate. They say, "Hello, Johnny," or "Hello, Bob. We still think of you. You're still with us. We never got over you, and we pray for you still, and we'll see you again. We'll all meet again." In a way, they represent us, these relatives and friends, and they speak for us as they walk among the headstones and remember. It's not so hard to summon memory, but it's hard to recapture meaning._
_And the living have a responsibility to remember the conditions that led to the wars in which our heroes died. Perhaps we can start by remembering this: that all of those who died for us and our country were, in one way or another, victims of a peace process that failed; victims of a decision to forget certain things; to forget, for instance, that the surest way to keep a peace going is to stay strong. Weakness, after all, is a temptation -- it tempts the pugnacious to assert themselves -- but strength is a declaration that cannot be misunderstood. Strength is a condition that declares actions have consequences. Strength is a prudent warning to the belligerent that aggression need not go unanswered._
_Peace fails when we forget what we stand for. It fails when we forget that our Republic is based on firm principles, principles that have real meaning, that with them, we are the last, best hope of man on Earth; without them, we're little more than the crust of a continent. Peace also fails when we forget to bring to the bargaining table God’s first intellectual gift to man: common sense. Common sense gives us a realistic knowledge of human beings and how they think, how they live in the world, what motivates them. Common sense tells us that man has magic in him, but also clay. Common sense can tell the difference between right and wrong. Common sense forgives error, but it always recognizes it to be error first._
_We endanger the peace and confuse all issues when we obscure the truth; when we refuse to name an act for what it is; when we refuse to see the obvious and seek safety in Almighty. Peace is only maintained and won by those who have clear eyes and brave minds. Peace is imperiled when we forget to try for agreements and settlements and treaties; when we forget to hold out our hands and strive; when we forget that God gave us talents to use in securing the ends He desires. Peace fails when we forget that agreements, once made, cannot be broken without a price._
_Each new day carries within it the potential for breakthroughs, for progress. Each new day bursts with possibilities. And so, hope is realistic and despair a pointless little sin. And peace fails when we forget to pray to the source of all peace and life and happiness. I think sometimes of General Matthew Ridgeway, who, the night before D-day, tossed sleepless on his cot and talked to the Lord and listened for the promise that God made to Joshua: “I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.”_
_We're surrounded today by the dead of our wars. We owe them a debt we can never repay. All we can do is remember them and what they did and why they had to be brave for us. All we can do is try to see that other young men never have to join them. Today, as never before, we must pledge to remember the things that will continue the peace. Today, as never before, we must pray for God's help in broadening and deepening the peace we enjoy. Let us pray for freedom and justice and a more stable world. And let us make a compact today with the dead, a promise in the words for which General Ridgeway listened, “I will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.”_
_In memory of those who gave the last full measure of devotion, may our efforts to achieve lasting peace gain strength. And through whatever coincidence or accident of timing, I tell you that a week from now when I am some thousands of miles away, believe me, the memory and the importance of this day will be in the forefront of my mind and in my heart._
_Thank you. God bless you all, and God bless America._
* * *

And through whatever coincidence or accident of timing, I tell you that a week from now when I am some thousands of miles away, believe me, the memory and the importance of this day will be in the forefront of my mind and in my heart. Thank you. God bless you all, and God bless America. | ©2017 My Faith Votes 10940 S Parker, 640, Parker, CO 80134

name: The White House
subject: Honoring Those Who Served
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce270664a1ed88a39325eaec46bdaf1af53d09a0c03469b5c357ffd386d8f2c82c7404a55c6fc572659673bcc64b352852

President Trump is working on behalf of a grateful nation to ensure that our veterans are properly recognized for their service and sacrifice. The President is initiating critical reforms at the VA, modernizing and expanding choice to healthcare, and expanding opportunities for veterans. Read more about how President Trump is putting our veterans first
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce37e07efc492936944a02499d1da66f16f5a5873663eb319095860b6cf749ee26f7e61e512a1204a05ea01153c583bdea
here .

“We will not rest until all of America’s great veterans can receive the care they so richly deserve. Tremendous progress has been made in a short period of time.”

- President Donald J. Trump

--------------------

President Trump Honors Veterans in Vietnam

This morning, in Da Nang, Vietnam, President Trump participated in a meet-and-greet with Vietnam War Veterans. The President expressed his gratitude for their service to the United States, and reaffirmed his commitment to honor the sacrifice of all Veterans and rebuild our military. After giving remarks, President Trump signed a proclamation to honor the veterans of the Vietnam War, part of an ongoing 13-year commemoration of their sacrifice for freedom.

“Our veterans are a national treasure, and I thank them all for their service, sacrifice, and patriotism. To each of you with me today, you are the heroes who fulfill your duty to our nation. And each of you, under the most difficult conditions, did what you had to do, and you did it well,” President Trump said.

Read the President's full remarks
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce2eb29574237985b816eadd9c2b96a6a0c48a8ee168210af88050987ba2690ccdfd573b81795c75b396819bea2da94d56
here.

Improving Services for American Vets

In honor of Veterans Day, Secretary of Veterans Affairs David Shulkin penned an op-ed in The Hill on recent developments in the Department of Veterans Affairs which will allow the department to provide services to veterans more effectively. These changes include improved internal accountability, same-day services for primary and mental health care, and an updated electronic health records system.

“This Veterans Day, we are redoubling our efforts to continue getting things done on behalf of those who have worn the uniform. Our veterans deserve no less,” Shulkin wrote.

Read the full op-ed
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce7fe622a3686db02965494a2958e6957ff4c4be283acbc61650cca6d09e8f517cbfc8c79f24d4276c9efc475a19a6391e
here.

On the Way to Historic Tax Reform

Yesterday's passage of the Tax Cuts and Jobs Act through the House Ways and Means Committee is an important step toward providing historic tax relief for the American people. President Trump remains committed to providing tax cuts for middle income families, a simplified tax code, and lower rates for American businesses. There is still much to do, but the Administration remains confident that, through continued cooperation with Congress, we are on the way to achieving these priorities this year.

Read more about tax reform
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce75d315fe6c60b465f2342bdcf368d63c38e09512fb7ef27596eba9a9a0fe596a36287fa438b2ccca31b9f676b6ad44b2
here.

Photo of the Day

President Donald J. Trump and First Lady Melania Trump at the National Assembly of the Republic of Korea | November 7, 2017 (Official White House Photo by Shealah Craighead)

POTUS TODAY

Tonight, President Trump will attend an APEC economic leaders meeting retreat in Da Nang, Vietnam, followed by a family photo and a working leaders luncheon. Later, the President will attend a second APEC economic leaders meeting retreat before departing Da Nang for Hanoi, Vietnam. After arriving in Hanoi, President Trump will attend a state banquet.

COMING UP

After concluding his visit to Vietnam, President Trump will travel to the Philippines, his last stop in Asia before returning to Washington, D.C. Tomorrow, Vice President Mike Pence will lead the annual Arlington National Cemetery Veterans Day ceremony.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=1c062d60a2a806ce8a4d7c273b2ca1cea2f3fe4f5450205fcd3eca35b2de724a72a4064ddaed1e936de739ef4cf862b4b8129a93476a3a89

Contact the White House |

name: Terry McAuliffe
subject: We can take what we did in VA nationwide >>
body:
Step up to help more Democrats win. Derek, I want to take a moment to send you my personal gratitude for the hard work our grassroots supporters put in to helping Governor-elect Ralph Northam, Lt. Governor-elect Justin Fairfax, and Attorney General Mark Herring win Tuesday's elections here in Virginia.

These victories, along with the 15 Virginia House of Delegates seats we flipped and major wins across the country, are the direct result of what happens when Democrats work together to win.

I can't overstate how thrilled my wife, Dorothy, and I are knowing we get to pass the baton to a family that will fight to protect our progress and work tirelessly for even more.

I know that Ralph and Justin will continue our hard work -- keeping our unemployment rate low while creating more jobs, further expanding access to affordable health care and the ballot box, and continuing to give our teachers everything they'll need to prepare our children for the future.

That's what happens when more Democrats win.

**We can take what the Democratic National Committee did in Virginia nationwide in 2018, Derek. And we should.

** Chip in $10 or more now to invest in smart technology, boots on the ground, and winning campaigns in all 50 states.

subject: One year later
body:
| | |
---
| | [ ](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=5ba7b5383f&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
|
---
|
---
| _**One Year Later**_ Looking back, it's hard to believe that this week marks one year since last year's historic elections. One year ago, we celebrated hard-earned Republican victories up and down the ballot. From **President Donald Trump's** historic win to big statewide and local victories, I know I'll always remember that big night and your efforts across the state that made it all possible.

Fast forward to this week, and Indiana is on a roll. The leaders we elected last November are hard at work for Hoosiers, working to make our nation and state better every day.

At the national level, **Vice President Mike Pence** was in Indiana yesterday with **U.S. Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta** to meet with Hoosier business leaders to discuss efforts to make our complex federal tax system more fair, more simple and far less burdensome. But that wasn't the only high-profile visit this week. **U.S. Attorney General Jeff Sessions** joined **Indiana Attorney General Curtis Hill** in Indianapolis on Monday to discuss taking the Ten Point Coalition's model nationwide.

At the state level this week, **Governor Eric Holcomb** unveiled his 2018 Next Level Agenda, laying out a vision to accelerate Indiana's momentum by focusing squarely on helping Hoosiers, from growing our economy and adding jobs, to strengthening our infrastructure and combating the drug epidemic. In addition to announcing his legislative agenda, Governor Holcomb also announced that Indiana has set another record, with more job commitments in 2017 than ever before in state history!

And at the local level, we had a caucus on Monday night to replace **Lloyd Arnold** in District 74's state representative seat, which includes Crawford, Dubois, Orange, Perry and Spencer counties, after he accepted the job as the Director of Law Enforcement for the Indiana Department of Natural Resources. We are excited to welcome **Steve Bartels** of Crawford County as Indiana's newest representative-elect!

It's been a great week for Indiana, and it's all because of the hard work you put in last year electing a rock star team of Republican leaders! I'd like to invite you to help celebrate this progress by joining me, Governor Holcomb and our statewide team one week from today for our 1st Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner. We'll be honoring our Hoosier veterans, including a special tribute to **Iraq War Veteran and Purple Heart recipient Josh Bleill** and **Medal of Honor recipient Sammy L. Davis** [Get more information and register here](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0f79cd180c&e=1acd9bb3d6).

Hope to see you next Friday!

-Kyle Hupfer

_Chairman Hupfer joins Indiana Democratic Party Chairman John Zody and Indiana Public Broadcasting reporter Brandon Smith __for the release of the Hoosier Survey, an annual survey of Hoosiers on hot button issues._
---
| |
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
---
|
---
---
| For two years running, Nov. 8th has been a significant day for **Governor Eric Holcomb**! After a historic 106-day campaign, Holcomb was elected as Indiana's 51st governor on Nov. 8, 2016, and this week, he unveiled his [2](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=3319e57e57&e=1acd9bb3d6)[018 Next Level Agenda](https://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=0210540ef9&e=1acd9bb3d6) on Nov. 8, 2017. The vision aims to continue Indiana's incredible momentum by:
* Cultivating a strong and diverse economy
* Maintaining and building the state’s infrastructure
* Developing a 21st century skilled and ready workforce
* Attacking the drug epidemic
* Delivering great government service

---
|
---
| | **_Congress of Counties- Save the Date_**
---
|
---
Save the date! The Indiana Republican Party will be hosting **Congress of Counties** , a two-day training event for party leaders, candidates and grassroots activists, on January 12-13 in Indianapolis. With breakout training sessions on topics like fundraising, data, technology, Get Out The Vote, communications, county party plans and much more, you won't want to miss this fun event!
|
---
|
---
---
| _**Vice President Pence Back Home in Indiana Yesterday**_
**Vice President Mike Pence** traveled back home to Indiana yesterday to discuss the need for federal tax reform. The **U.S. Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta** joined the VP and Hoosiers in Plainfield to ramp up support for passing tax reform.

The House Republican plan -- unveiled just last week and supported by **President Trump** and **Vice President Pence** \-- aims to put more money into the pockets of hardworking middle-class Hoosiers by simplifying our tax code, creating jobs and making American businesses more competitive. We've done it in Indiana, and now it's time to take the Hoosier model to Washington.

_(Official White House photos by Joyce Boghosian)_
---
|
---

clean_body: | | | | | | | | One Year Later Looking back, it's hard to believe that this week marks one year since last year's historic elections. One year ago, we celebrated hard-earned Republican victories up and down the ballot. From President Donald Trump's historic win to big statewide and local victories, I know I'll always remember that big night and your efforts across the state that made it all possible. Fast forward to this week, and Indiana is on a roll. The leaders we elected last November are hard at work for Hoosiers, working to make our nation and state better every day. At the national level, Vice President Mike Pence was in Indiana yesterday with U.S. Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta to meet with Hoosier business leaders to discuss efforts to make our complex federal tax system more fair, more simple and far less burdensome. But that wasn't the only high-profile visit this week. U.S. Attorney General Jeff Sessions joined Indiana Attorney General Curtis Hill in Indianapolis on Monday to discuss taking the Ten Point Coalition's model nationwide. At the state level this week, Governor Eric Holcomb unveiled his 2018 Next Level Agenda, laying out a vision to accelerate Indiana's momentum by focusing squarely on helping Hoosiers, from growing our economy and adding jobs, to strengthening our infrastructure and combating the drug epidemic. In addition to announcing his legislative agenda, Governor Holcomb also announced that Indiana has set another record, with more job commitments in 2017 than ever before in state history! And at the local level, we had a caucus on Monday night to replace Lloyd Arnold in District 74's state representative seat, which includes Crawford, Dubois, Orange, Perry and Spencer counties, after he accepted the job as the Director of Law Enforcement for the Indiana Department of Natural Resources. We are excited to welcome Steve Bartels of Crawford County as Indiana's newest representative-elect! It's been a great week for Indiana, and it's all because of the hard work you put in last year electing a rock star team of Republican leaders! I'd like to invite you to help celebrate this progress by joining me, Governor Holcomb and our statewide team one week from today for our 1st Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner. We'll be honoring our Hoosier veterans, including a special tribute to Iraq War Veteran and Purple Heart recipient Josh Bleill and Medal of Honor recipient Sammy L. Davis . Hope to see you next Friday! -Kyle Hupfer Chairman Hupfer joins Indiana Democratic Party Chairman John Zody and Indiana Public Broadcasting reporter Brandon Smith for the release of the Hoosier Survey, an annual survey of Hoosiers on hot button issues. | | | | Governor Holcomb's Week That Was | | | For two years running, Nov. 8th has been a significant day for Governor Eric Holcomb! After a historic 106-day campaign, Holcomb was elected as Indiana's 51st governor on Nov. 8, 2016, and this week, he unveiled his on Nov. 8, 2017. The vision aims to continue Indiana's incredible momentum by: Cultivating a strong and diverse economy Maintaining and building the state’s infrastructure Developing a 21st century skilled and ready workforce Attacking the drug epidemic Delivering great government service | | | Congress of Counties- Save the Date | Save the date! The Indiana Republican Party will be hosting Congress of Counties , a two-day training event for party leaders, candidates and grassroots activists, on January 12-13 in Indianapolis. With breakout training sessions on topics like fundraising, data, technology, Get Out The Vote, communications, county party plans and much more, you won't want to miss this fun event! | | | Vice President Pence Back Home in Indiana Yesterday Vice President Mike Pence traveled back home to Indiana yesterday to discuss the need for federal tax reform. The U.S. Secretary of Labor Alexander Acosta joined the VP and Hoosiers in Plainfield to ramp up support for passing tax reform. The House Republican plan -- unveiled just last week and supported by President Trump and Vice President Pence \-- aims to put more money into the pockets of hardworking middle-class Hoosiers by simplifying our tax code, creating jobs and making American businesses more competitive. We've done it in Indiana, and now it's time to take the Hoosier model to Washington. (Official White House photos by Joyce Boghosian)_ | | News You Can Use Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch Congressman Jim Banks Congressman Todd Rokita Congresswoman Susan Brooks Congressman Luke Messer Congressman Larry Bucshon Auditor Tera Klutz Treasurer Kelly Mitchell Events Calendar \- Harrison County Young Republican Mini Golf Fun Night \- First Annual Team Holcomb Fall Dinner \- Hendricks County Chairman's Breakfast \- SMCC Holiday Reception \- Fayette County GOP Christmas Dinner & Celebration | | | | On Monday of this week the 74th House District elected Steve Bartels to fill the vacancy created by State Representative Lloyd Arnold’s recent resignation. | "Read More" | | U.S. Attorney General Jeff Sessions with Indiana Attorney General Curtis Hill and members of the Ten Point Coalition to discuss ways to reduce violent crime in Indianapolis. | "Read More" | | On Wednesday, the Governor and IEDC a record-breaking year for Indiana, as the Hoosier state in 2017 saw companies commit to create nearly 29,000 new Hoosier jobs in the coming years! | "Read More" | | | | | | | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | ---|--- | | | | | | | | | | | | | | mailto:info@indiana.gop | This email was sent to mailto:dpwillis67@gmail.com Indiana Republican State Committee, Inc. 101 W. **NH REPUBLICAN PARTY****WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH** **_A Note From Jeanie_** **Dear Derek,**
**Today I had the privilege of attending the 242nd birthday celebration of the Marine Corps in Manchester. Senator Kelly Ayotte gave a moving speech to a packed room of Marines, family members, and supporters. Representative Al Baldasaro read a proclamation from the NH House of Representatives. We were all reminded that military service demands a special kind of sacrifice.

Tomorrow is Veterans Day, a day that all across our great country, Americans are pausing to honor Veterans through public ceremonies and private prayer. Here in New Hampshire, I will be attending the Veterans Day Breakfast at Liberty House and later speaking at a Veterans ceremony. With NH Veterans representing 9% of the state’s total population, I hope you will take time to honor our Veterans too.

Looking to 2018, I’m pleased to let you know that the NHGOP will launch NHGOPVETS, an outreach and recognition program. If you have an interest in getting involved, please give us a call at 225-9341.

To all those who fought for our freedom, covered the midnight watch, fed the troops, maintained the supply lines, fueled the planes, comforted the wounded and guarded the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier...our flag flies higher and prouder because you served. Thank you!**
**Sincerely, **
**Jeanie** **Join the Grassroots Circle!**

**The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party.**
**Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. clean_body: nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTYWEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, NOVEMBER 10TH A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, Today I had the privilege of attending the 242nd birthday celebration of the Marine Corps in Manchester. Senator Kelly Ayotte gave a moving speech to a packed room of Marines, family members, and supporters. Representative Al Baldasaro read a proclamation from the NH House of Representatives. We were all reminded that military service demands a special kind of sacrifice. Tomorrow is Veterans Day, a day that all across our great country, Americans are pausing to honor Veterans through public ceremonies and private prayer. Here in New Hampshire, I will be attending the Veterans Day Breakfast at Liberty House and later speaking at a Veterans ceremony. With NH Veterans representing 9% of the state’s total population, I hope you will take time to honor our Veterans too. Looking to 2018, I’m pleased to let you know that the NHGOP will launch NHGOPVETS, an outreach and recognition program. If you have an interest in getting involved, please give us a call at 225-9341. To all those who fought for our freedom, covered the midnight watch, fed the troops, maintained the supply lines, fueled the planes, comforted the wounded and guarded the Tomb of the Unknown Soldier...our flag flies higher and prouder because you served. Thank you! Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor. Intern with the NH GOP! Are you a student or know a student that is interested in Politics?** Learn the skills necessary to win elections and meet the Republican leaders that govern our State and write legislation. FEATURED EVENTS UPCOMING EVENTS Manchester GOP November Meeting November 13 @ 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm Region 5 R.E.D. Town Hall with Guest Speaker Senate President Chuck Morse November 13 @ 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm CD1 Candidate Forum November 13 @ 7:00 pm - 8:30 pm RED Town Hall – Epping November 14 @ 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm Bedford Republican Committee Meeting November 14 @ 7:00 pm - 8:00 pm MCR Monthly Meeting November 15 @ 6:00 pm - 7:00 pm PHOTO OF THE WEEK Governor Chris Sununu joined by parents, advocates, and children to talk about Education Savings Accounts earlier this week! REPUBLICAN SPOTLIGHT This week, we want to recognize all Veterans, regardless of partisanship. We would not be able to engage in the debates we have without the sacrifices all Veterans have made, and we need to recognize that this Veterans Day. ** Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | _**What They're Saying:**_
_"Hoosiers are already seeing the positive results of Governor Holcomb’s collaborative, consensus-building approach this past legislative session. As we continue to focus on strengthening our economy, infrastructure, workforce, attacking the drug epidemic, and delivering great government service, our 2018 Next Level Agenda charts a bold course toward a bright future for our state."_
**-Lt. Governor Suzanne Crouch**

_"We are happy to be a part of Governor Holcomb's education efforts, and any efforts that promise to bring more success to students is something we will stand behind.”_
**-Superintendent of Public Instruction Jennifer McCormick**

_"I applaud the governor for bringing an aggressive and well-thought-out agenda to the table. The Senate continues to believe that workforce development is a critical issue for our state, and the governor’s agenda reflects that priority._ "
-**Senate President Pro Tem David Long**

_"Indiana’s strong economy continues to pay dividends through record job creation and low unemployment. To maintain this momentum, and to continue to attract employers in record numbers, it’s clear we have to strengthen our workforce pipeline. The governor has put forth a bold agenda, and we look forward to working with him on workforce and other critical issues facing our state and finding the best solutions for all Hoosiers."
-_**Indiana Speaker of the House Brian Bosma**

_“We see evidence everyday of Indiana’s economic and job accomplishments. This agenda – particularly clarifying software-as-a-service (SaaS) tax treatment, increased management of our water resources, accelerating efforts on autonomous vehicles and expanding STEM opportunities through increased computer science education – properly looks toward the future and will help ensure those successes continue.”_
**Kevin Brinegar, President & CEO, Indiana Chamber of Commerce**

_"The governor’s commitment to building a skilled and ready workforce is a big deal to small business..**.**.NFIB supports the governor’s efforts, and we’ll do whatever we can to help the governor’s plans become reality.”

name: Delegate-elect Lee Carter
subject: I wouldn't be here without you
body:
#
Friends,
My name is Lee Carter, and I just won a race that no one ever thought was possible. Thanks to your dedication to progressive candidates such as myself, I will be sworn in next year as the next Delegate for Virginia's 50th district.
While many thought my victory was impossible, we were able to prove them wrong because of your commitment to the political revolution. As a Marine, this belief of service has been guided by the core values of the Marine Corps -- honor, courage, and commitment. This idea of serving others is not unique to the Marines, we learn it from watching others as children and adults. Recognizing the service of those to our country on Veteran's Day is critical in developing the values that can improve the lives of Americans.
Whether you are a veteran, a relative or friend of one, or someone who believes that our higher purpose in life is to help one another, we are united to a common goal. For me, the most important value I hope to instill in others is to put people over profits. It's this belief -- instilled in me witnessing my father's service in the Navy -- that inspired me to serve as a Marine, and now in the Virginia House of Delegates.
While I was on active duty in Kuwait in 2009, I was injured by a 400lb crate of radios that dropped on my leg. The container sliced my leg so deep that I could see the muscle. As a Marine, I was able to get treatment and the time I needed to recover quickly. As a private citizen in Virginia, when I suffered a workplace injury, I was not as lucky. I could barely walk for months and I was worried every day how I was going to provide for my family. Once I could walk again, I decided I should not just walk, but run for office so that others would not have to go through what I endured.
This Veteran's Day I hope we all pause to recognize the service that so many have made for our country and the values that their service has inspired us to hold deeply. To those of you that have served, thank you. And to those of you that recognize that service, I hope you are inspired as I was to become more involved. To organize for a better future. And to work together to accomplish what others think is impossible.
What happened on Tuesday is just the beginning. Your role in reshaping our politics and winning more races is critical and I hope you will continue supporting candidates that support the political revolution.
In solidarity,
Lee Carter
Delegate-elect, Virginia 50
Our Revolution Member

WASHINGTON, DC, 20002

Email is one of the most important tools we have to reach supporters like you, but you can let us know if you'd like to [receive fewer emails](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae7d/bed43eb/2f735a18/75ff4a62/3611226941/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjIyMTIzIn0=/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/s/OR-fewer-emails?source=email_footer&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=22123"). We'd hate to see you go, but if you need to do so, click here to [unsubscribe](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae7d/bed43eb/2f735a18/75ff4a6d/3611226941/VEsF/ "https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/unsubscribe/"). Help us continue transforming American politics and fighting for progressive candidates and causes by [contributing to Our Revolution here](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7ae7d/bed43eb/2f735a18/75ff4a6c/3611226941/VEsC/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/ourrevolution?refcode=email-footer-OR").
